clues.clue: Inspired by burrs clinging to his dog, George de Mestral invented this hook-and-loop fastener.
A weak 3M adhesive led Art Fry to create this repositionable sticky note.
Gideon Sundback perfected this interlocking-teeth clothing fastener around 1913.
Hungarian journalist Laszlo Biro patented this smear-resistant pen in the 1930s.
Percy Spencer invented this oven after a radar magnetron melted a candy bar in his pocket.
Reese's Cups pair chocolate with this creamy filling.
Each Ferrero Rocher hides a whole one of these round tree nuts at its center.
Whoppers and Maltesers share this airy, toasted-grain center.
A Cadbury Creme Egg's fondant is dyed to mimic this golden part of a real egg.
Junior Mints and York patties share this cool, dark flavor.
Our solar system resides in this barred-spiral galaxy.
The violent death of a massive star produces this brilliant explosion.
A region of spacetime from which not even light can escape is this.
Betelgeuse is a red one of these aging, enormous stars destined to explode.
On a collision course with the Milky Way, this is the nearest large spiral galaxy (M31).
A widely cited Alaska law bans waking this hibernating animal to take a photo.
An 1897 Indiana bill nearly set this circle-ratio constant to 3.2 by statute.
Old 'blue laws' in many U.S. towns reputedly forbid selling this on Sundays.
A reputed Gainesville, Georgia ordinance requires this fried bird be eaten only by hand.
An old Vermont law reputedly required a wife to have her husband's permission to wear these dental prosthetics.
Samuel Morse co-developed this dot-and-dash signaling code.
Johannes Gutenberg's 15th-century movable-type machine is generally called this.
Guglielmo Marconi pioneered the long-distance wireless transmission of this.
Tim Berners-Lee proposed this hyperlinked information system in 1989.
clues.response: What is the World Wide Web?

clues.clue: Bell's famous first telephone sentence was a summons to this lab assistant.

final.clue: These chocolate-and-peanut-butter cups regularly top surveys of favorite trick-or-treat candy.
